definition: The 2s10s yield curve steepening is approximately 70-80% driven by term premium expansion, not an economic recovery signal. ACM decomposition shows expected-rate differential contributed ~25bp of steepening while term premium contributed ~80-100bp.
evidence: Term-premium-driven steepening is correlation-positive; expectations-driven is historically correlation-neutral-to-negative
Labeled 'single most operationally useful claim' in pair_3 debate
Identifies a concrete misinterpretation likely widespread among market participants
open_questions: Attribution could be 60-85% depending on model choice
Directional conclusion is robust but precise magnitude is model-dependent
How should duration positioning differ between term-premium and expectations-driven steepening?
